source.title: Staged docs for Spending by Geography

observations.statement: The documented endpoint is POST /api/v2/search/spending_by_geography/ on https://api.usaspending.gov.
The endpoint takes award filters and returns aggregated obligation amounts in different geographic areas.

Top-level documented request fields are filters (required), scope (required), geo_layer (required), subawards (optional boolean), spending_level (optional enum with default transactions), and geo_layer_filters (optional array of geography codes).
Documented scope values are place_of_performance and recipient_location, and documented geo_layer values are state, county, district, and country.

source.title: AdvancedFilterObject in Spending by Geography docs

observations.statement: The filters object reuses AdvancedFilterObject and documents these filter members: keywords, description, time_period, place_of_performance_scope, place_of_performance_locations, agencies, recipient_search_text, recipient_scope, recipient_locations, recipient_type_names, award_type_codes, award_ids, award_amounts, program_numbers, naics_codes, tas_codes, psc_codes, contract_pricing_type_codes, set_aside_type_codes, extent_competed_type_codes, treasury_account_components, object_class, program_activity, program_activities, and def_codes.
place_of_performance_scope and recipient_scope document default domestic only for county, district, and state geo layers; both also support foreign.

The time_period object is polymorphic: if spending_level is subawards or subawards is true, it uses the subaward search time-period shape; otherwise it uses the transaction search time-period shape from shared search_filters documentation.
AgencyObject requires type, tier, and name; toptier_name is optional and only applicable when tier is subtier.
ProgramActivityObject requires at least one of name or code when used.

AwardAmounts supports lower_bound and upper_bound; NAICSCodeObject supports require and exclude; PSCCodeObject and TASCodeObject support require and exclude arrays-of-arrays; TreasuryAccountComponentsObject requires aid and main and optionally ata, bpoa, epoa, a, and sub.
Award IDs wrapped in double quotes are documented to perform exact matches instead of fuzzier full text matching.
If def_codes are in the COVID-19 or IIJA group, the docs say only transactions on or after the enactment date of the associated public law are returned.

source.title: Documented response for Spending by Geography

observations.statement: The documented 200 response includes top-level scope, geo_layer, spending_level, results, and optional messages.

Each documented GeographyTypeResult has shape_code, aggregated_amount, display_name, population, and per_capita; total_outlays is optional and only included when the response spending_level is awards.

The sample response shows warning-style messages, including a time-period earliest-date limitation for search and a deprecation warning telling callers to prefer spending_level=subawards over the top-level subawards flag.

source.title: Current raw profile path for spending by geography

observations.statement: No current raw MCP profile content was present for this slug in the loaded endpoint context.
Because no current raw profile is available, the current MCP surface does not expose any confirmed request fields for this endpoint from the legacy profile pipeline.

source.title: Derived coverage ledger for spending by geography

observations.statement: Important request surface absent from the current raw profile includes the required filters, scope, and geo_layer fields plus optional spending_level, geo_layer_filters, and subawards controls.
This endpoint is an aggregate geography summary surface rather than a paginated detail listing; the docs do not describe page, limit, or sort controls in the response or request.
The geography output is grouped by a selected geo_layer and scope, so results are suitable for choropleths or per-capita comparisons but not for award-level drilldown without using another endpoint.

source.title: Live probe for spending by geography baseline request

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
The 'subawards' field will be deprecated in the future. Set 'spending_level' to 'subawards' instead. See documentation for more information.

observations.statement: A POST with filters.time_period, scope=place_of_performance, geo_layer=state, and geo_layer_filters=[CA] returned HTTP 200 and a JSON body.
When spending_level was omitted, the live response reported spending_level=transactions.
The live response echoed scope and geo_layer and returned one result row for CA with shape_code, display_name, aggregated_amount, population, and per_capita.
The live response included the documented warning messages about the 2007-10-01 search floor and the planned deprecation of the subawards field.

source.title: Live probe for spending by geography awards request

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
The 'subawards' field will be deprecated in the future. Set 'spending_level' to 'subawards' instead. See documentation for more information.

observations.statement: A request with spending_level=awards returned HTTP 200 and echoed spending_level=awards.
The awards-level result row included total_outlays, matching the documented conditional field behavior.
The awards-level response kept the same top-level shape and warning messages as the transaction-level baseline probe.
